description_long: This is a ceramic vase with a bulbous body, a tall cylindrical neck, and two symmetrical handles extending from the shoulder to the rim. The surface is decorated with hand-painted dark blue or black abstract motifs, possibly floral or symbolic, on a pale, slightly off-white background. The rim appears uneven and shows signs of chipping, indicating age or wear. The handles are robust and slightly curved, providing both functional grip and visual balance. The glaze is matte with some visible imperfections and discoloration, suggesting it may be an antique or handmade piece. The overall form is traditional, evoking Mediterranean or Middle Eastern pottery styles.
description: A hand-painted ceramic vase with two curved handles, a tall neck, and abstract blue motifs on a pale background. The surface is matte and shows signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing both handles, the tall neck, and the main painted motif on the body.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by one handle, with less visible decoration and more of the vase's curvature exposed.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with a different section of the painted motif and visible chips on the rim.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the other handle, showing more of the vase's plain surface and subtle glaze variations.
